pull.title: Revert "cmake: configure libsecp256k1.pc during install"
pull.body:
This reverts commit 7106dce6fd410043fc297139c6307cb6016d2a7b.

This causes a regression for packaging, as the generated `.pc` file will contain the location that was used to build the package archive.

milestone.description: See https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues/32275 for release schedule of Bitcoin Core 30.0

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

events.message:
Revert "cmake: configure libsecp256k1.pc during install"

This reverts commit 7106dce6fd410043fc297139c6307cb6016d2a7b.

This causes a regression for packaging, as the generated
`.pc` file will contain the location that was used to build
the package archive.

events.body: That's unfortunate, yeah. For anyone else, see @purpleKarrot's comment in #1692  for more context.
